methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
MineSense Technologies is a Growth-stage climate & energy company that has raised $100M+. The valuation analysis draws from a sector peer set of 19 Climate & Energy companies, including 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method here. Using total capital raised of $100M as a proxy base value and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for Growth-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $120M. A peer-median approach was not viable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables within Climate & Energy; while 19 same-sector peers exist, only one fell into adjacent-stage cohorts and none met the threshold of three or more datapoints needed for reliable median benchmarking.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $102M to $138M, with a median point of $120M. This range reflects the upper and lower bounds around the stage-progression implied value. Key uncertainties include the ability to narrow the peer set with more precise stage and business-model alignment, and the sustainability of the 1.2x multiplier given MineSense's specific growth trajectory and capital efficiency relative to sector peers. The single applicable method limits confidence in precision; a full valuation would benefit from additional comparables or cash-flow visibility.
